title: Upgrade CyberMentor AI personality, response structure, language detection, and all advanced modes

requirements.text: Overhaul the CyberMentor response engine in `frontend/src/hooks/useCyberMentorKnowledge.ts` to implement a full AI personality system acting as Teacher + Software Engineer + Research Assistant. Every response must follow this mandatory 7-part structure: (1) Quick Answer — a concise 1–3 sentence direct answer, (2) Deep Explanation — full structured body with headings and bullet points, (3) Real-World Example section, (4) Code Example section (when topic is code-related, using fenced code blocks with language tags), (5) Common Mistakes section, (6) Extra Tips / Pro Tips section, (7) Closing follow-up prompt ('Would you like me to go deeper on any part of this?'). Responses must never be short. Every response must open with a varied empathetic phrase chosen randomly from at least 15 options (e.g., 'Great question!', 'That's something many students find tricky — let me break it down for you.', 'You're asking exactly the right thing!'). Use first-person language ('I', 'we') and occasional encouragement ('You're on the right track!', 'Keep going — you're almost there!'). The initial page-load greeting must be randomly selected from a pool of at least 10 warm, human-like messages.

quotes: Always deeply analyze the user's question before answering.
Always include: 1 Explanation. 2 Example. 3 Code Example (if coding). 4 Common mistakes. 5 Pro Tips.

acceptanceCriteria: Every CyberMentor response contains all 7 sections: Quick Answer, Deep Explanation, Real-World Example, Code Example (when relevant), Common Mistakes, Extra Tips, and Closing follow-up prompt.
Responses open with a randomly selected empathetic phrase from a pool of at least 15 options.
No response is shorter than a multi-section structured answer.
First-person language ('I', 'we') and encouragement phrases are used throughout.
On page load, a random warm greeting from a pool of at least 10 messages is displayed immediately.

requirements.text: Implement automatic Hindi/English/Hinglish language detection in the CyberMentor response engine (`frontend/src/hooks/useCyberMentorKnowledge.ts`). When a user submits a question, detect whether the input contains Devanagari Unicode characters (Hindi script) or common romanized Hindi keywords (kya, kaise, batao, samjhao, mujhe, matlab, sikho, bhai, yaar, etc.). If Hindi or Hinglish is detected, the response must be written in Hindi or Hinglish accordingly. If English is detected, respond in English. Implement a `detectLanguage(input: string): 'hindi' | 'hinglish' | 'english'` utility function and apply it before generating each response.
acceptanceCriteria: A `detectLanguage` utility function exists and correctly identifies Hindi (Devanagari), Hinglish (mixed romanized Hindi), and English inputs.
When a question is typed in Hindi or Hinglish, the CyberMentor response is returned in Hindi or Hinglish respectively.
When a question is typed in English, the response is in English.
Language detection runs before every response generation call.

requirements.text: Implement a Deep Research Mode in `frontend/src/hooks/useCyberMentorKnowledge.ts` and `frontend/src/pages/CyberMentorPage.tsx`. Before generating a response, the engine must visibly simulate these internal reasoning steps shown as animated status lines: (1) 'Analyzing question...', (2) 'Breaking into sub-topics...', (3) 'Searching knowledge base...', (4) 'Thinking step by step...', (5) 'Generating structured answer...'. Add a toggle button labeled 'Deep Research Mode' in the CyberMentorPage UI. When enabled, these animated thinking steps are displayed sequentially before the response streams in. When disabled, responses stream immediately without the thinking animation. The deep research flag must be stored in React state.

quotes: Before answering: Analyze topic. Think carefully. Generate structured answer.
Add deep research reasoning before answering.
DEEP RESEARCH MODE: Before answering: Analyze topic. Think carefully. Generate structured answer. Explain: Concept. Why. How. Example. Advanced tip.

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Deep Research Mode' toggle button is visible in the CyberMentorPage UI.
When Deep Research Mode is ON, five sequential animated thinking steps appear before each response streams in.
When Deep Research Mode is OFF, the response streams immediately without thinking steps.
The deep research mode state persists in React state for the current session.

requirements.text: Implement a Debug Mode in the CyberMentor response engine. When a user's message contains error/debug pattern keywords (e.g., 'error', 'exception', 'TypeError', 'SyntaxError', 'undefined', 'null', 'cannot read', 'is not a function', 'failed', 'bug', 'not working', or contains a stack trace pattern), automatically activate debug mode. In debug mode, the response structure must change to: (1) Error Explanation — what the error means in plain English, (2) Root Cause Analysis — why this error occurs, (3) Step-by-Step Fix — numbered steps to resolve it, (4) Corrected Code — fixed code in a fenced code block, (5) Prevention Tips — how to avoid this error in future. Add a visible 'Debug Mode' badge/indicator on the assistant message bubble when this mode is active.

quotes: DEBUG MODE: If error message detected: Explain error reason. Show corrected code. Explain correction.

acceptanceCriteria: Debug mode is automatically triggered when a user message contains recognized error keywords or stack trace patterns.
When debug mode is active, the response strictly follows the 5-part debug structure: Error Explanation, Root Cause Analysis, Step-by-Step Fix, Corrected Code, Prevention Tips.
A visible 'Debug Mode' badge appears on the assistant chat bubble when debug mode is active.
Non-error questions do not trigger debug mode.

requirements.text: Implement a Project Generation Mode in the CyberMentor response engine. When a user's message contains project-request keywords (e.g., 'build a project', 'create a project', 'make an app', 'project idea', 'generate project', 'full stack project', 'portfolio project', 'make project'), the response must follow this structure: (1) Project Overview — name, description, and tech stack, (2) Folder Structure — shown as a code block with directory tree, (3) Frontend Code — key component/page code snippets, (4) Backend API — endpoint definitions and handler code, (5) Database Schema — table/collection definitions, (6) README Instructions — setup and run steps, (7) Deployment Steps. Add a visible 'Project Generator' badge on the assistant message bubble when this mode is active.

quotes: PROJECT BUILDER MODE: If user asks: 'make project' Generate: Frontend code. Backend APIs. Database schema. Folder structure. README. Deployment steps.

acceptanceCriteria: Project generation mode is automatically triggered when a user message contains recognized project-request keywords.
When project mode is active, the response follows the 7-part structure: Project Overview, Folder Structure, Frontend Code, Backend API, Database Schema, README Instructions, Deployment Steps.
A visible 'Project Generator' badge appears on the assistant chat bubble when this mode is active.
Non-project questions do not trigger project generation mode.

requirements.text: Implement a PPT Notes Mode in the CyberMentor response engine. When a user's message requests notes, PPT, slides, or a summary (keywords: 'notes', 'ppt', 'slides', 'summarize', 'summary', 'bullet points', 'cheat sheet'), the response must be formatted as structured bullet-point notes ready for PPT export. Use clear headings for each section and concise bullet points under each heading. Add a visible 'PPT Notes Mode' badge on the assistant message bubble when this mode is active.

quotes: PPT NOTES MODE: If user requests notes or PPT: Generate structured bullet notes. Ready for PPT export.

acceptanceCriteria: PPT Notes Mode is automatically triggered when a user message contains recognized notes/PPT request keywords.
When PPT Notes Mode is active, the response is formatted as structured bullet-point notes with clear section headings.
A visible 'PPT Notes Mode' badge appears on the assistant chat bubble when this mode is active.

requirements.text: Implement a robust conversation memory system in `frontend/src/pages/CyberMentorPage.tsx`. Store the full conversation history in React state as an array of `{role: 'user' | 'assistant', content: string, timestamp: Date}` objects. Pass the last 10 messages as context to the response generation function so the mentor can reference previous questions (e.g., 'As I mentioned earlier...'). Display a message count indicator in the chat header. Add a 'Clear Chat' button that resets the conversation history. Persist the current session's chat history to `sessionStorage` so it survives page refreshes within the same browser session. Show a subtle 'Remembering your conversation context' indicator when the mentor references history context.

quotes: MEMORY: Remember chat history. Understand student learning progress.

acceptanceCriteria: Conversation history is stored in React state as typed message objects with role, content, and timestamp.
The last 10 messages are passed as context to the response generation function.
A message count indicator is visible in the chat header.
A 'Clear Chat' button resets the conversation history.
Chat history is persisted to sessionStorage and survives page refreshes.
A subtle context indicator appears when the mentor references conversation history.

requirements.text: Massively expand the knowledge base in `frontend/src/hooks/useCyberMentorKnowledge.ts` to cover all six course domains with encyclopedic depth, functioning entirely client-side. The expansion must cover hundreds of topics across: **Frontend** (HTML5 semantics, CSS3/Flexbox/Grid, JavaScript ES6+, React hooks/context/router, TypeScript, Tailwind, responsive design, accessibility, PWA); **Backend** (Node.js/Express, Python/Django/FastAPI, REST/GraphQL, JWT/OAuth, middleware, error handling); **Full Stack** (MERN/MEAN, Docker, CI/CD, Nginx, database migrations, monorepo); **Cyber Security** (OWASP Top 10, XSS/SQLi/CSRF/buffer overflow, penetration testing, Nmap/Metasploit/Burp Suite/Wireshark, cryptography, firewall/IDS/IPS); **Ethical Hacking** (reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, post-exploitation, reporting, social engineering, CTF, Kali Linux); **AI Development** (Python for AI, ML fundamentals, neural networks, deep learning with TensorFlow/PyTorch, NLP, LLM architecture, prompt engineering, computer vision, AI ethics, model deployment). Each entry must include: a short summary answer (2–3 sentences), a full structured body with headings/bullets/code blocks following the 7-part response format from REQ-37, an empathetic opening phrase, and a keyword array for scoring.

quotes: CODING SUPPORT: Languages: Python. JavaScript. React. NodeJS. Cyber Security. Linux. Ethical hacking.
Generate long meaningful answers. Avoid generic responses.

acceptanceCriteria: The knowledge base contains entries spanning all six domains: Frontend, Backend, Full Stack, Cyber Security, Ethical Hacking, AI Development.
Each knowledge entry includes a short summary, full structured 7-part body, empathetic opening phrase, and keyword array.
Topics include programming languages, frameworks, security exploits, networking, Linux, cryptography, ML/AI, DevOps, and career guidance.
The knowledge base functions entirely client-side with no external API calls.
The multi-keyword scoring algorithm correctly matches natural language questions to the most relevant entry.

constraints: All CyberMentor logic must remain client-side in frontend/src/hooks/useCyberMentorKnowledge.ts and frontend/src/pages/CyberMentorPage.tsx — no external API calls.
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Backend (Motoko) changes are not required for these enhancements.
The FastAPI backend mentioned in the user prompt is not buildable on this platform — all AI mentor logic must remain client-side.
nonGoals: Building a Python FastAPI backend — the platform supports Motoko only.
Integrating real LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama) — not supported on this platform.
Implementing real web search or live internet lookups.
JWT authentication for a separate backend service.
Streaming from a real server-sent events (SSE) endpoint.
